steps.title: Track the contrast
steps.content: The narrator says Hans Castorp is ordinary, but the story itself is very much worth telling. The parenthetical adds that the story is his and that not every story happens to everybody, suggesting that the valuable story gives its subject some significance.

steps.title: Reject distortions
steps.content: A says the story's interest is difficult to understand. B makes age and manner of telling indicators of importance rather than consequences of temporal distance. C treats the story as typical of inconsequential lives. The remaining choice captures a valuable story happening to an ordinary person. The answer is D.
